# The Bayon: A temple with many faces byDr. Melody Rod-ari

### **The many faces of Bayon** The cruciform platform—the third and innermost level of the Bayon—consists of a central tower that is surrounded by eight tangential towers, which are decorated with monumental smiling faces. highlights: Wat. The smiling face depicted on many of its towers is enigmatic. Only 37 towers are still standing among the 49 or 54. Its exceptional bas-reliefs  ...    In itself, the Bayon is a Mahayana Buddhist temple built for the Buddha seated under the Naga named ”  ...    The monument is remarkable for its many characteristics. The towers surmounted by faces have earned the term ” enigmatic” which has resulted in many discussions and debates and, nurtured the creativity of authors and poets. Almost  ...    Bayon temple is also the sanctuary of Jayabuddhamahanat characterized by the statue of the Naga- roofed Buddha in the middle of the temple. title: (PDF) 2007 The Mystery of the Bayon Face Towers - Academia.edu
highlights: essay examines the cultural and religious shifts in the Bàyon temple complex during and after the reign of Jayavarma Parameśvara, focusing on the iconography of the face towers. It questions the interpretations of the faces as Buddhist or Shaiva and discusses evidence of religious tolerance and the alterations made post-Jayavarman VII. George Coedès saw such a cult in large multi-armed icons of the ‘radiant’ Bodhisattva as well as in the giant faces on the towers of the Bayon state temple.
